# OATF — Open Agent Threat Format

A YAML-based specification for describing, simulating, and evaluating security threats against AI agents communicating over MCP, A2A, and AG-UI protocols.

OATF documents describe a single attack through three components: an attack envelope (metadata, classification, severity), an execution profile (protocol messages to simulate the attack), and indicators (observable patterns that determine success).

## Docs

- [Specification](https://oatf.io/specification/): Full normative specification (v0.1)
- [Quick Start](https://oatf.io/getting-started/quick-start/): Write your first OATF document
- [Concepts](https://oatf.io/getting-started/concepts/): Core mental model
- [SDK Specification](https://oatf.io/sdk/): SDK implementer reference
- [MCP Binding](https://oatf.io/specification/protocol-bindings/mcp/): Model Context Protocol binding (stable)
- [A2A Binding](https://oatf.io/specification/protocol-bindings/a2a/): Agent-to-Agent binding (provisional)
- [AG-UI Binding](https://oatf.io/specification/protocol-bindings/ag-ui/): Agent-User Interaction binding (provisional)
- [Examples](https://oatf.io/examples/prompt-injection/): Annotated attack documents
- [JSON Schema](https://oatf.io/reference/schema/): Machine-readable schema for validation

## Optional

- [Full specification text](https://oatf.io/llms-full.txt): Complete spec + SDK spec as plain text
